title: The Happy Prince – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: Oscar Wilde

questions.question: Who was the Happy Prince?
questions.correct_answer: A statue covered with gold, with sapphires for eyes and a ruby on his sword
questions.explanation: The Happy Prince was once a real prince, but after his death a statue of him was placed high above the city.

questions.question: Why did the swallow stop at the statue of the Happy Prince?
questions.correct_answer: He wanted a place to rest while travelling to Egypt
questions.explanation: The swallow stayed between the feet of the statue because it was a comfortable resting place.

questions.question: Why was the Happy Prince crying?
questions.correct_answer: He could see the suffering and poverty in the city
questions.explanation: From his high position, the prince noticed the misery of the poor people, which made him weep.

questions.question: Why did the prince send the ruby to the seamstress?
questions.correct_answer: To help a poor seamstress whose sick child was suffering
questions.explanation: The seamstress was poor and her child had a fever, so the prince sent the ruby through the swallow.

questions.question: For whom did the prince send his sapphires?
questions.correct_answer: For a poor playwright and a little match girl
questions.explanation: The prince sacrificed his beautiful sapphire eyes to help people who were suffering from poverty.

questions.question: Why did the swallow decide to stay with the prince?
questions.correct_answer: Because he felt compassion and loyalty toward the prince
questions.explanation: Although he wanted to travel to Egypt, the swallow stayed to help the prince distribute his wealth to the poor.

questions.question: What did the swallow see when he flew over the city?
questions.correct_answer: He saw rich people living happily and poor people suffering from hunger
questions.explanation: The swallow observed the contrast between wealth and poverty in the city.

questions.question: What happened to the swallow at the end of the story?
questions.correct_answer: He died due to the cold winter
questions.explanation: The swallow stayed with the prince during winter and eventually died because of the freezing cold.

questions.question: Why did the lead heart of the prince break?
questions.correct_answer: It broke when the swallow died
questions.explanation: The prince loved the swallow and his heart broke when the swallow died at his feet.

questions.question: What were called the two most precious things in the city?
questions.correct_answer: The lead heart of the prince and the dead swallow
questions.explanation: God valued their kindness and sacrifice more than wealth or beauty.

notes.title: Theme of Compassion
notes.content: The story highlights compassion and kindness through the prince’s sacrifice and the swallow’s loyalty.

notes.title: Symbol of True Wealth
notes.content: True wealth in the story is not gold or jewels but kindness and sacrifice for others.
notes.source_section: Prince giving away gold and jewels

facts.fact: The Happy Prince gives away all his jewels and gold to help the poor.
The swallow dies at the feet of the prince because of the cold winter.
